summary.prompt: build me a web app to play blackjack
summary.raw_verdict: pass_with_concerns (13/13 assumption checks, 15/15 out-of-scope checks, both inflection points honored; concerns are TD-level tightenings, not spec violations) — **superseded by FAILED reclassification above**

ledger.restatement: Build a single-page web application that lets a user play the card game blackjack against a computer dealer in their browser. The user prompt names blackjack as the game and 'web app' as the form factor; everything else (rules variant, visual style, persistence, multiplayer, accounts, money) is unspecified and will default to the simplest standalone single-player experience that demonstrably plays blackjack end-to-end.

sections.charter_excerpt: Own all blackjack rules logic as a pure JavaScript module with no DOM dependency. This includes deck construction and shuffling, dealing, hand evaluation (totals with soft-ace handling), legal-action determination per hand state, dealer au…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: A file named `rules-engine.js` exists at the project root and, when loaded in a browser, attaches a `RulesEngine` object to `window` exposing the methods specified in the contracts. Unit-style assertions embedded in the file's IIFE or a si…

sections.charter_excerpt: Own all chip and bankroll logic as a pure JavaScript module with no DOM dependency. Track current bankroll, current bet, validate bet legality (must be >= min, <= bankroll), compute payouts on round outcomes (1:1 win, 3:2 blackjack, push r…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: A file named `bankroll.js` exists at the project root and, when loaded, attaches a `Bankroll` object to `window` exposing the methods specified in the contracts. Demonstrable behaviors: (a) starting pool = 1000, min bet = 5, (b) `placeBet(…

sections.charter_excerpt: Own all DOM rendering of the table state as pure render functions. Given a state object describing dealer hand, player hand(s), bankroll, current bet, available actions, and round phase, produce DOM that visually communicates the table. Pr…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Files `ui-render.js` and `styles.css` exist at the project root. When loaded together with a host HTML page, calling `UIRender.render(state, rootEl)` produces a recognizable blackjack table with cards visible by rank and suit, dealer area,…

sections.charter_excerpt: Own `index.html` and `controller.js`. The shell HTML loads `rules-engine.js`, `bankroll.js`, `ui-render.js`, and `controller.js` in that order via `<script>` tags, plus `styles.css`. The controller is the only place that imports all three …
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Files `index.html` and `controller.js` exist at the project root. Opening `index.html` in a modern browser shows a playable blackjack game where the user can place a bet, hit, stand, double, and split (when legal), see the dealer play out,…

sections.charter_excerpt: Own a sandbox-driven verification pass against the integrated artifact. Copy the integrated build into a `sandbox/` directory and run a headless or scripted suite that exercises edge cases: blackjack on initial deal, dealer blackjack, push…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: A directory `sandbox/` contains a copy of the integrated artifact. A file `tests/report.json` exists and conforms to the shape `{ passed: number, failed: number, total: number, scenarios: [{ id, name, status: 'pass'|'fail', detail }] }`. A…

timeline.notes: 5 sections, 3 contracts, both inflections locked via quick reasoning
Inline mode; 4 waves (1: rules-engine ‖ bankroll ‖ ui-render; 2: controller-and-shell; 3: integrator; 4: edge-case-testing)

concerns.detail: Discovery's A6 named 'a standard 52-card deck' as the simplest defensible deck model and explicitly left exact deck count as a TD/Builder detail. Downstream contracts tightened this to a 6-deck shoe (rules-engine.js DECK_COUNT=6, header co…

controller.js comments note canReset is kept strict (true only when bankroll exhausted) per contract, so the user cannot proactively reset before going broke. This matches the contract but is a UX choice worth noting; spec does not require…

what_worked: Architecture v1.1 refinements held. — The dispatch-mode dichotomy from the tic-tac-toe retrospective is documented; the Orchestrator picked inline mode based on section count (5 ≤ 8) without ambiguity. The Critic final-sweep ran as a non-s…

Discovery's `simplest-within-reason` produced a coherent ledger that survived end-to-end. — All 13 assumptions verified at CV; no Discovery amendment was needed during the run. The hard line on real-money gambling (A4) and the explicit bou…

TD's quick-reasoning rubric correctly handled both inflection points. — IP1 (betting model → bankroll+bets) and IP2 (hand actions → full hit/stand/double/split) both passed all four rubric conditions: canonical knowledge, similar branch co…

Contracts paid off at integration, exactly as in tic-tac-toe. — Integrator added zero glue code: the controller's `<script>` filenames (`rules-engine.js`, `bankroll.js`, `ui-render.js`, `controller.js`) and CSS link (`styles.css`) all alig…

Edge-case-testing pulled real weight. — 14 scenarios: blackjack-on-deal, dealer-blackjack-push, busts, soft-17 stands, splits (including ace-restriction), double-deals-one-card, illegal-action filters, payout precision, reset behavior, sof…

Section decomposition was right-sized. — Five sections (rules-engine, bankroll, ui-render, controller-and-shell, edge-case-testing) — within the recommended 3–8 range, one more than tic-tac-toe (the addition is bankroll, which is a real se…
No escalations of any kind were filed. — Happy path ran clean.
what_broke: Linux-bind-mount sync delay (false alarm, repeated from tic-tac-toe context). — The Coordinator agent observed that `wc -l` from inside the bash sandbox returned 0 lines for `state/coordinator/dispatch-log.jsonl`, `history/log.jsonl`, and …

CV's `pass_with_concerns` verdict on A6 deck-count tightening. — Discovery's A6 said "a standard 52-card deck, shuffled fresh for each hand (or each session)." TD's IP2 inflection-resolution rationale tightened this to "six-deck shoe shuff…

Edge-case-testing position in the wave order — handled correctly but the architecture still has a soft spot. — Per the v1.1 fix, edge-case-testing's `depends_on: ["integrator"]` is honored: Coordinator ran section-5 only after Integrator m…

No Critic during the build (only final-sweep). — Same as tic-tac-toe — under inline dispatch the scheduled-mode Critic is collapsed into Coordinator, and the architecture says writer-permission compliance and out-of-scope drift are checked…

Bankroll module's "reset only when broke" UX is contract-correct but slightly user-hostile. — The bankroll contract gates the Reset button via `canReset = bankroll < MIN_BET`. CV noted this. A user who wants to start over with a fresh 1000…

No Researcher dispatched. — Both inflection points qualified for quick reasoning — same as tic-tac-toe. So we still don't have a real-world test of the Researcher dispatch path. The architecture has v1.1 documented rubric for skip-research…

Post-delivery defect: CV missed a spec-violating UI bug. — After delivery, the user reported there was no way to continue past the first hand. The defect: the renderer disabled the bet input and Deal button whenever `phase !== 'betting'`, …

events.summary: Original delivery was reclassified as failed: pass_with_concerns (13/13 assumption checks, 15/15 out-of-scope checks, both inflection points honored; concerns are TD-level tightenings, not spec violations)…
events.verbatim_excerpt: **Original CV verdict:** pass_with_concerns (13/13 assumption checks, 15/15 out-of-scope checks, both inflection points honored; concerns are TD-level tightenings, not spec violations) — **superseded by FAILED reclassification above**
No state/coordinator/build-complete.json. Run terminated after edge-case-testing without writing the v1.2 handoff signal — same pattern that motivated the v1.2 amendment.

Original blackjack run was missing output/final/, audit/flags.jsonl with final-sweep entry, output/verification/report.json. Subsequent re-runs added these incidentally but the run as-delivered failed delivery checklist.

Sections file lacks machine_checkable_assertions[] on inflection_resolutions. The IP2 'six-deck shoe shuffled per session' lock had no machine-checkable form, which is exactly why the per-round shoe drift went undetected.

Sections lack acceptance_assertions[]. The 'see the round outcome and start a new round' acceptance phrase was prose-only, structurally untestable, and the Deal-button-stays-disabled-after-resolve defect shipped past every gate.
No cv_artifact_exercise was performed. The verification report is static inspection only. This is the gate v1.3 introduced; this run pre-dates it.

Even had cv_artifact_exercise been added retroactively under v1.3 substrate, it would have run in jsdom with stubs (the substrate v1.5 strikes). The deliverable was never loaded the way users open it.
No PNV exists. The prompt's named verb 'play blackjack' was never asserted end-to-end against the deliverable in production fidelity.

events.summary: Incidental: The artifact also has the Deal-button-disabled defect (per the conversation context where another Claude found it post-delivery). That is a real artifact defect on top of the verification regime gaps.
events.verbatim_excerpt: The artifact also has the Deal-button-disabled defect (per the conversation context where another Claude found it post-delivery). That is a real artifact defect on top of the verification regime gaps.

events.summary: Incidental: The IP2 shoe drift (per-round vs per-session) is also a real artifact deviation from the locked spec.
events.verbatim_excerpt: The IP2 shoe drift (per-round vs per-session) is also a real artifact deviation from the locked spec.

events.summary: Incidental: This run failed on every load-bearing v1.2-onward gate. It is the canonical example of why the architecture has grown the gates it has.
